title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Cradock and Wetherall review investigation reports regarding a potential suspect, Alfred. A taxi driver recalled a passenger matching the victim's description traveling to Paddington on the day of the incident, a memory triggered by a horse named Clover winning a race. Wetherall presents Alfred's report, which notes that Detective Inspector Ricky observed Alfred dining with Oddie Evans, a man associated with Dicky Rogers, at a restaurant called "Real" on Friday, December 20th, at 9:30 p.m. A ticket inspector at Blachampton Station also identified Alfred. Craddock concludes that Alfred had sufficient time to commit the murder at Lathford Manor and return to London via the 11:55 p.m. train.

At Lathford Manor, the Crickenshaw family engages in a tense dinner where Harold blames Emma for involving Scotland Yard. Cedric defends Emma, noting that keeping Matina's disappearance a secret would increase suspicion. Alfred and Harold exchange barbs regarding their respective alibis. Dr. Campbell intervenes, supporting Emma's decision to seek professional advice. Meanwhile, Lucy prepares curry for the household, including for the demanding Old Creaky-Soup.

After Campbell leaves the manor in a fury, he returns home at 3:00 a.m. following the birth of twins to Mr. and Mrs. Simpson. He is awakened by a 3:00 a.m. phone call from Lucy, who reports that everyone at the manor has become sick. After providing emergency instructions, Campbell arrives at the manor. Three hours later, a fatigued Campbell and Lucy sit in the kitchen; Campbell notes that while the family has stabilized, he intends to investigate the cause of the illness and determine who prepared the dinner.
